subject_output:
# `content/levels/fault_lines_07_the_chasm.yaml`

Since the level needs to ship as a single new file in `content/levels/`, here it is, written against the schema described in `gdd/content/levels.md` (axis-aligned `kind: solid` platforms vs. `kind: ledge` for the narrow ones; integer tile coordinates with `+y` up; hazards as rects; deterministic-friendly numeric placement so replays don't drift).

```yaml
# content/levels/fault_lines_07_the_chasm.yaml
#
# Region:   fault_lines
# Tier:     3
# Slot:     07  (seventh fault-lines level)
# Intent:   A vertical shaft descent. The player commits to a fall, uses
#           glide to drift and dash to course-correct, threading six
#           platforms (three wide, three narrow ledges) on a zig-zag
#           natural path. Two checkpoints break the shaft into thirds.
#           Four ember pickups (2 ember each) sit on the natural path so
#           a clean descent leaves the ember bright; hesitating burns
#           glide drain faster than pickups replenish. Bottom is lava;
#           overshooting the exit alcove kills.
#
# Coords:   1 unit = 1 tile (~moth height). +x right, +y up.
# Bounds:   x in [0, 24], y in [0, 120].

id: fault_lines_07_the_chasm
name: "The Chasm"
region: fault_lines
tier: 3
order: 7

# Camera & world bounds for the shaft.
bounds:
  min: [0, 0]
  max: [24, 120]

# Ambient cave is darker than caverns; ember light cone matters more here.
ambient_light: 0.06

# Spawn on a tiny entry shelf at the top-left of the shaft.
spawn:
  position: [3, 116]
  facing: right

# Exit alcove at the bottom-left, just above the lava line.
exit:
  position: [3, 11]
  size: [4, 3]

# --- Shaft walls (axis-aligned solids forming the chimney) -------------
geometry:
  solids:
    # Left wall, full height
    - id: wall_left
      kind: solid
      rect: [0, 0, 1, 120]      # [x, y, w, h], origin = bottom-left
    # Right wall, full height
    - id: wall_right
      kind: solid
      rect: [23, 0, 1, 120]
    # Ceiling cap above spawn
    - id: ceiling
      kind: solid
      rect: [1, 119, 22, 1]
    # Entry shelf the player spawns on (left side, top)
    - id: entry_shelf
      kind: solid
      rect: [1, 113, 5, 1]
    # Floor of the exit alcove (bottom-left)
    - id: exit_floor
      kind: solid
      rect: [1, 10, 6, 1]
    # Lip on the right side of the exit alcove so the alcove is a real
    # pocket — overshooting horizontally drops you straight into lava.
    - id: exit_lip
      kind: solid
      rect: [6, 10, 1, 3]

  # --- Six descent platforms ------------------------------------------
  # Zig-zag: right, left, right, left, right, left.
  # Three "solid" (wide, forgiving) and three "ledge" (narrow, ~2 tiles).
  platforms:
    # P1 — wide axis-aligned, right side. Roughly one jump-arc-fall down
    # from the entry shelf if the player drifts right with glide.
    - id: p1_wide_right
      kind: solid
      rect: [14, 100, 8, 1]

    # P2 — narrow ledge, left side. Requires either a dash left or a
    # glide-drift from P1. Hosts checkpoint 1.
    - id: p2_ledge_left
      kind: ledge
      rect: [4, 86, 2, 1]

    # P3 — wide axis-aligned, right side. Natural fall-and-drift target.
    - id: p3_wide_right
      kind: solid
      rect: [15, 72, 7, 1]

    # P4 — narrow ledge, left side. Tightest landing in the level; the
    # player has just refilled ember at pickup 2 and may dash here.
    - id: p4_ledge_left
      kind: ledge
      rect: [3, 58, 2, 1]

    # P5 — wide axis-aligned, right side. Hosts checkpoint 2.
    - id: p5_wide_right
      kind: solid
      rect: [13, 44, 7, 1]

    # P6 — narrow ledge, left side. Last stop before the exit alcove.
    - id: p6_ledge_left
      kind: ledge
      rect: [3, 30, 2, 1]

# --- Checkpoints (split the shaft into thirds along the natural path) --
checkpoints:
  - id: cp1
    position: [5, 87]            # standing on P2
    platform: p2_ledge_left
  - id: cp2
    position: [16, 45]           # standing on P5
    platform: p5_wide_right

# --- Ember pickups (4 × 2 ember = 8 total) -----------------------------
# Placed mid-air on the natural zig-zag so a clean descent collects them
# without detours. A hesitating player misses pickups AND drains glide.
pickups:
  - id: em1
    kind: ember
    value: 2
    position: [13, 108]          # between entry shelf and P1
  - id: em2
    kind: ember
    value: 2
    position: [12, 79]           # between P2 and P3
  - id: em3
    kind: ember
    value: 2
    position: [12, 51]           # between P4 and P5
  - id: em4
    kind: ember
    value: 2
    position: [10, 22]           # between P6 and the exit alcove

# --- Hazards -----------------------------------------------------------
# Lava floods the bottom of the shaft. The exit alcove floor sits at
# y=10–11; lava surface is y=8. Overshoot the alcove horizontally or
# vertically and you die instantly, respawning at cp2 (or cp1, or
# spawn) per the standard "cheap death, sticky progress" rule.
hazards:
  - id: lava_pool
    kind: lava
    rect: [1, 0, 22, 8]

# --- Designer notes (non-runtime, kept for the level review pass) ------
notes: |
  Pacing target: ~25–35 s skilled descent, ~2–4 min first-timer.
  This is the shortest fault-lines level by design — it's a pure
  commitment test before fault_lines_08 reintroduces crushers.

  Skilled line: spawn → glide-drift right onto P1 (collect em1 in
  flight) → short hop + dash left to P2 (touch cp1) → fall right onto
  P3 (collect em2) → dash left to P4 → fall right onto P5 (collect
  em3, touch cp2) → dash/drift left onto P6 → final glide left into
  the exit alcove (collect em4 on the way).

  Failure modes the layout intentionally rewards:
    * Hesitating on a ledge dims the ember; the next gap is then
      harder to read, encouraging continuous motion.
    * Over-dashing past P2/P4/P6 drops the player one tier down at
      worst (back to the previous checkpoint), never to lava, except
      from the P6→exit segment where overshoot is fatal — that's the
      one moment the level asks for a committed glide instead of a
      dash.
```

### Why this layout matches the brief

- **Six platforms across the shaft, mixed kinds.** P1/P3/P5 are wide axis-aligned `solid` platforms; P2/P4/P6 are 2-tile-wide `ledge` platforms. They alternate sides (right/left/right/left/right/left), which is what makes the descent a "chasm" rather than a stack.
- **Two checkpoints on the natural path.** `cp1` sits on P2 (≈⅓ down), `cp2` on P5 (≈⅔ down) — both on platforms the player must land on anyway, so they can't be skipped.
- **Four ember pickups totaling 8.** Each pickup is `value: 2`, placed mid-air between consecutive landings so a flowing descent grabs them without diverting; a stalling player misses them *and* spends more ember on glide drain — reinforcing the *vision through motion* pillar.
- **Lava below the exit.** `lava_pool` covers `y ∈ [0,8]` across the full shaft width. The exit alcove (`exit_floor` + `exit_lip`) is a pocket at `y=10–13` on the left wall; overshooting it horizontally drops the moth straight into lava, which is the lethal-overshoot the brief calls for.
- **Three-action vocabulary respected.** No wall-clings, no double jumps, no combat triggers; the only mechanics referenced are spawn/exit/platforms/checkpoints/pickups/hazards. Determinism is preserved because every coordinate is an integer and there are no RNG-driven entities.

      rect: [13, 44, 7, 1]

    - id: p6_ledge_left

# --- Checkpoints (split the shaft into thirds along the natural path) --
checkpoints:
  - id: cp1
    position: [5, 87]            # standing on P2
    platform: p2_ledge_left
  - id: cp2
    position: [16, 45]           # standing on P5
    platform: p5_wide_right

# --- Ember pickups (4 × 2 ember = 8 total) -----------------------------
# Placed mid-air on the natural zig-zag so a clean descent collects them
# without detours. A hesitating player misses pickups AND drains glide.
pickups:
  - id: em1
    kind: ember
    value: 2
    position: [13, 108]          # between entry shelf and P1
  - id: em2
    kind: ember
    value: 2
    position: [12, 79]           # between P2 and P3
  - id: em3
    kind: ember
    value: 2
    position: [12, 51]           # between P4 and P5
  - id: em4

    position: [10, 22]           # between P6 and the exit alcove

# --- Hazards -----------------------------------------------------------

  flight) → short hop + dash left to P2 (touch cp1) → fall right onto

intent_score.rationale: The output perfectly fulfills all requirements of the task brief while adhering to the design brief's core pillars. It implements exactly six platforms (three wide solids, three narrow ledges), two checkpoints on the natural path, and four ember pickups totaling 8 units. The level design reinforces the 'Vision through motion' pillar by placing pickups in the flight path and using a lava hazard to punish overshooting the exit.
